objective: Standardize structured agent cycle plans org-wide by moving the canonical schema and validator into governance, wiring reusable CI enforcement, and making structured JSON the shared source of truth for governed issue execution.
scope_boundary: This slice is limited to governance-owned schema, validation, reusable CI enforcement, and shared standards/docs updates.
Markdown plan notes may remain as companion artifacts, but only structured JSON is treated as canonical in this slice.
This slice may create a follow-up issue if repo-level migration or rollout communication still needs explicit tracking after governance-side standardization lands.
out_of_scope: Backfilling every historical plan artifact across all repos.
Changing product behavior outside governance, CI, and planning-contract surfaces.
Inventing a second competing schema in any product repo.
research_summary: HealthcarePlatform already demonstrated a stronger planning pattern with structured agent cycle plans, specialist review data, and alternate-model review captured in machine-readable JSON. Governance, however, still lacked an org-wide canonical schema or reusable validator, which meant other repos could not adopt the same pattern consistently. The right org-wide move is therefore to place the schema and validator in governance, wire reusable governance CI to enforce it on issue/riskfix execution branches, and update shared standards so structured JSON becomes the canonical plan artifact across governed repos.

sprints.name: Sprint 1
sprints.goal: Create the governance-owned canonical schema and validator.
tasks: Add a canonical structured-agent-cycle-plan JSON schema under docs/schemas.
Add a governance validator script that can scan repos for structured plan artifacts.
Ensure the schema captures specialist reviews, alternate-model review, and execution handoff.
acceptance_criteria: Governance owns the canonical schema file.
The validator can pass on valid structured plan files and fail missing/malformed required fields.
The schema shape aligns with the proven HealthcarePlatform plan pattern.

sprints.name: Sprint 2
sprints.goal: Wire reusable enforcement and shared documentation.
tasks: Update reusable governance CI to validate structured plan files for issue/riskfix branches.
Update STANDARDS.md and README.md so structured JSON is the canonical org-wide plan artifact.
Update governance feature/fail-fast/backlog docs to reflect the new contract.
acceptance_criteria: Reusable governance CI can validate structured plan files before execution-ready branches proceed.
The shared standards contract names structured JSON as canonical and Markdown as companion-only.
The governance repo documents the new capability and why it exists.

specialist_reviews.focus: Validate that the governance-owned schema captures the actual structured review/handoff fields already proven in HealthcarePlatform.
specialist_reviews.summary: The shared schema should preserve specialist_reviews, alternate_model_review, execution_handoff, and material_deviation_rules as first-class fields. Those are the useful pieces that make the plan machine-checkable instead of just another JSON wrapper.

specialist_reviews.focus: Validate that the shared workflow can enforce the new contract org-wide without requiring each repo to invent a separate validator.
specialist_reviews.summary: The correct place for the validator is governance-check.yml, with the governance repo checked out as the source of the shared validator when other repos run the reusable workflow.

alternate_model_review.summary: This slice standardizes shared governance mechanics and does not require a separate alternate-model review to proceed.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implementation is approved for governance-owned schema/validator/CI/docs changes and the minimum follow-up issue creation needed if repo rollout work remains after review.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Validate the schema and reusable workflow against this branch’s own structured plan artifact, then create any explicit rollout follow-up needed before merge.

material_deviation_rules: If reusable CI cannot validate plans in non-governance repos without additional repo-specific code, stop and issue-back the rollout gap instead of hiding it.
If the schema needs repo-specific branches or shapes to fit one repo, stop and amend the shared contract rather than duplicating schemas.
If Review shows repo-adoption communication or migration still needs explicit tracking, create a follow-up issue before closing.
